income_thresholds.description: No specific income threshold; must be a member of BPL (below poverty line) family or belong to identified occupation group in the unorganized sector
income_thresholds.source: AABY Guidelines / LIC of India / Ministry of Finance

exclusion_rules.description: Only individuals aged 18-59 are eligible for enrollment as primary member
Only head of family or one earning member per BPL family can be enrolled
Must belong to BPL family or be in an identified unorganized sector occupation group
Persons already covered under employer-provided life/accident insurance are excluded

family_criteria.family_definition: One member per BPL family — either head of family or one earning member. The insured member's children (up to 2, classes 9-12) eligible for scholarship benefit.

covered_procedures: Natural death benefit: Rs 30,000 lump sum to nominee
Accidental death benefit: Rs 75,000 lump sum to nominee
Permanent total disability (accident): Rs 75,000 lump sum
Partial permanent disability (accident — loss of one eye or one limb): Rs 37,500 lump sum
Scholarship for children: Rs 100/month per child for up to 2 children studying in classes 9-12
excluded_procedures: Death or disability from self-inflicted injury
Death or disability while under influence of intoxicants
Death or disability from war, riot, or nuclear hazard

required_documents.name: BPL Certificate / BPL Ration Card
alternatives: Identification by nodal agency of the occupation group
required_documents.local_name_hi: बीपीएल प्रमाण पत्र / बीपीएल राशन कार्ड
required_documents.notes: Proof of BPL status or certification from identified occupation group's nodal agency

required_documents.name: Age Proof
required_documents.notes: Must prove age between 18-59 years at enrollment

required_documents.name: Nominee Details
required_documents.notes: Name, age, relationship, and Aadhaar of nominee for death/disability benefit claim

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For scheme communication and claim status updates

enrollment_channels: Nodal agency of identified occupation group
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Identify if you belong to a BPL family or identified unorganized sector occupation group
Step 2: Contact nearest nodal agency (occupation group body) or LIC branch office
Step 3: Submit enrollment form with Aadhaar, BPL certificate, age proof, photographs, and nominee details
Step 4: Nodal agency verifies BPL/occupation status and submits master list to LIC
Step 5: Annual premium of Rs 200 paid — 50% by Central Government from Social Security Fund, 50% by State Government
Step 6: LIC issues insurance policy; member receives certificate of insurance
Step 7: In case of claim — nominee submits death/disability certificate and claim form to LIC via nodal agency
description_for_embedding: Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana (AABY) is a central government social security insurance scheme administered by LIC of India for BPL families and unorganized sector workers aged 18-59 years. It provides life and accident insurance: Rs 30,000 for natural death, Rs 75,000 for accidental death, Rs 75,000 for permanent total disability, and Rs 37,500 for partial permanent disability. Additionally, children of insured members (up to 2 studying in classes 9-12) receive a scholarship of Rs 100 per month. The premium of Rs 200 per year is shared equally between Central and State Governments — completely free for the beneficiary. Only the head of family or one earning member per BPL household can be enrolled. This is not a health insurance scheme — it covers death and disability benefits only.
